content: No country spends money for education, public and private, so lavishly as does the United States.
options: The United States is the most wasteful country in terms of education.
The United States makes the most generous investment in education in the world.
The United States has the most lavish universities in the world.
Education in the United States is the most expensive in the world.
explanation: 原句意为没有国家在教育上的投入能像美国一样大手笔，lavishly表示慷慨大量地投入，对应B选项“美国在全球教育领域投入最为慷慨”。

content: Our society’s confidence in its institutions of higher education is expressed through investments that wisely couple support for research with support for graduate education.
options: Our society attaches greater importance to research than to graduate education.
A large proportion of the national budget goes to the development of education.
Our investments in education combine support for research with support for graduate education.
Our society’s lack of confidence in its institutions of higher education derives from generous investments made in education.
explanation: couple A with B 意为将A与B结合，原句说明教育投资兼顾科研与研究生教育，和C选项表述完全一致。

content: The medieval image of the university as an ivory tower… is an image that has been superseded by the modern university constructed not of ivory, but of a highly porous material…
options: Hi-tech materials have been widely applied to modern universities.
Different from the medieval image of a university, a modern university is more open.
The university in the medieval times was made of very expensive materials.
The medieval image of the university as an ivory tower was made of a highly porous material.
explanation: ivory tower象牙塔指代封闭隔绝的学府，porous material多孔材料比喻开放包容，原句表示现代大学取代了封闭的中世纪象牙塔形象，即现代大学更加开放。

content: The search for new ideas and knowledge is not and cannot be motivated by utilitarian concerns.
options: The search for new ideas and knowledge shouldn’t be driven by practical consideration.
The search for new ideas and knowledge is the priority concern of the United States.
The search for new ideas and knowledge should be based on practical consideration.
Young people in the United States are highly motivated to search for new ideas and knowledge.
explanation: utilitarian concerns指功利、实用层面的考量，原句表示探索新知不能由功利目的驱动，与A选项同义。

content: When the Nobel laureate John Nash developed the mathematical concepts underlying non-cooperative game theory as a graduate student at Princeton, he could not foresee that those concepts would be used today to analyze election strategies and the causes of war.
options: Nash was awarded the Nobel Prize because he analyzed election strategies and the causes of war.
Nash was awarded the Nobel Prize because of the many applications of game theory.
When Nash developed his mathematical concepts, he himself had no idea that they would be used today for many other purposes.
Nash found many applications of his mathematical concepts underlying game theory.
explanation: could not foresee意为无法预见，原句指纳什当年创立理论时，并未料到该理论如今会有诸多其他用途，对应C选项。

content: …, for new knowledge often depends upon preparing fertile ground in obscure places where serendipity and good luck, as well as deep intelligence, can sprout.
options: New knowledge entirely depends on good luck.
New knowledge is often found by luck but a creative environment is required for luck to flourish.
New knowledge may derive from the fields where scientists of intelligence cluster.
Good luck, rather than intelligence, plays a central role in searching for new knowledge.
explanation: fertile ground沃土比喻适宜创新的环境，serendipity意外好运，原句说明新知诞生需要孕育机遇的良好环境，对应B。

content: I hope that our country's decision-makers will draw on the knowledge that resides on our campuses…
options: I hope the decision-makers will visit our campus.
I hope the decision-makers will return to campus to recharge themselves with new knowledge.
I hope the decision-makers will take full advantage of the knowledge in academic circles.
I hope the decision-makers will be pace-setters of colleges and universities.
explanation: draw on意为利用、汲取，resides on our campuses指代校园学术界的知识，原句希望决策者充分利用学界知识，对应C。

content: What we should seek to impart in our college, is not so much learning itself as the spirit of learning.
options: We don’t expect our students to learn much.
We try to impart more learning to our students.
What we advocate first in our college is the spirit of learning rather than learning itself.
We seek to separate the learning process from the spirit of learning.
explanation: not so much A as B 表示“与其说是A，不如说是B；更看重B而非A”，原句说明大学更应传授求知精神而非单纯知识，对应C。

content: It consists… in the habit of catholic observation and a preference for the non-partisan point of view…
options: It consists in the habit of religious observation and a preference for political affiliation.
It consists in the habit of careful observation and a preference for reasoning.
It consists in the habit of observation in varied areas and a preference for an impartial point of view.
It consists in the habit of observation like a catholic and a preference for an insightful viewpoint.
explanation: catholic此处含义是广泛全面的，non-partisan无党派的、中立客观的；C选项中varied areas对应广泛观察，impartial point of view对应中立视角。

content: Wilson, and the presidents who followed him, rejected the narrow idea of a liberal arts education as preparation for a profession.
options: Wilson believed that a liberal arts education will prepare students better for a profession.
Wilson thought the preparation of students for a profession was narrow-minded.
Wilson didn’t think preparation for a profession was the only purpose of a liberal arts education.
Wilson criticized bitterly the idea of a liberal arts education as preparation for a profession.
explanation: rejected the narrow idea意为否定这种狭隘观点——把博雅教育仅当作职业准备，即威尔逊认为职业准备并非博雅教育的唯一目的，选C。
